race.name: Gran Fondo Florida
race.tagline: Rolling hills and quiet country roads through Central Florida's horse farm country

vitals.field_size: Unknown; waves of 25 riders every 5 minutes starting 8:00 AM
vitals.entry_fee: 2025 pricing: Gran $150–$200 (escalating); Medio $95–$145; Piccolo varies. 2026 pricing not yet confirmed.
vitals.cutoff_time: 10 mph minimum progression speed (includes stopped time); riders below threshold directed to shorter route at pre-established splits

terrain.surface: Paved roads (public roads; obey traffic laws)

fondo_rating.scoring_notes: Migrated from gravel-race-automation. Original score: 64. adventure dimension dropped, road_surface added. Score recalculated: 63.

suffering_zones: Rolling climbs in mid-course; moderate sustained elevation gain
Technical rolling terrain; potential for fatigue in final 20 miles of Gran route
Potential for competitive efforts; timed segment may occur here

logistics.lodging_strategy: Dade City and surrounding Pasco County offer hotels, motels, and vacation rentals; no event-specific lodging partnerships reported

notable_moments: Hosted 2025 USA Cycling Gran Fondo National Championships
Annual fixture in USA Cycling Gran Fondo National Series since at least 2025
Festival atmosphere with street closures, live music, raffle, and happy hour

biased_opinion.summary: Gran Fondo Florida is a well-executed, accessible gran fondo that balances competitive timed segments with a welcoming festival atmosphere. The rolling Central Florida terrain and quiet country roads offer a pleasant early-season ride without extreme technical or elevation demands. Ideal for riders seeking national series points and a supportive community event rather than a hardcore racing experience. The 100-mile distance and 3,248 feet of elevation are substantial but not punishing. March weather is excellent. Primary drawbacks: field size unknown, exact distance ambiguous, and prestige limited to regional/national series level rather than international recognition.

final_verdict.one_liner: Recommended for intermediate to advanced road cyclists seeking an organized, community-focused gran fondo with national series credentials. Best suited for early-season training and age-group competition. Not recommended for riders seeking extreme terrain, elite-level racing, or exotic destinations.

videos.description: Short clip of the Dade City Gran Fondo in Dade City great weather and very fun ride in the hills

videos.title: Suarez Gran Fondo National Series: Freezing Start, Fast Pace & Epic Roads
videos.description:
This last weekend I did the Suarez Gan Fondo National Series in Dade City Florida. We formed a team of 7 cyclist and took the challenge of riding 98 miles. 

Follow Me ⬇️⬇️⬇️
🚴🏼 STRAVA | https://www.strava.com/athletes/1132250
📸 INSTAGRAM | https://www.instagram.com/theridexper...
🤨 FACEBOOK | https://www.facebook.com/TheRideXperi...
——————————————————————————————-

🎥 Insta360You can use the code INRICN9 to help support the channel or thought this link: https://store.insta360.com/end-of-season?utm_term=INRICN9

Regino Bike & Co.: http://reginobike.com
The Ride Xperience: http://theridexperience.com
Business inquiries: contact@theridexperience.com
Gran Fondo National Series: https://www.granfondonationalseries.com/gran-fondo-national-championship

videos.curation_reason: Recent first-person race experience with detailed course info and good production quality from established cycling channel.

videos.title: Gran Fondo Florida: Blazing Speeds on a Flat, Windy Course!
videos.description:
Riding my road bike on one more Gran Fondo in Florida. This GFNY race was super fast and what made it even harder was the wind.  

Follow Me ⬇️⬇️⬇️
🚴🏼 STRAVA | https://www.strava.com/athletes/1132250
📸 INSTAGRAM | https://www.instagram.com/theridexper...
🤨 FACEBOOK | https://www.facebook.com/TheRideXperi...
——————————————————————————————-

🎥 Insta360You can use the code INRICN9 to help support the channel or thought these links: 

https://store.insta360.com/product/x4?c=3395&utm_term=INRICN9

Mount: https://store.insta360.com/product/bike-computer-mount?utm_term=INRICN9

Regino Bike & Co.: http://reginobike.com
The Ride Xperience: http://theridexperience.com
Business inquiries: contact@theridexperience.com
GFNY: https://gfny.com/

videos.title: Gran Fondo Survival Guide
videos.description:
Four tips on how to make mass start events, like Gran Fondos and Sportives a memorable and enjoyable event.

Special thanks to my friend Pembroke Kenner for providing some coverage of Fondo Riding at the METRO ATLANTA CYCLING CLUB One Love Century.
https://youtu.be/fuTqkET7Gko
videos.transcript:
hey what's up everybody welcome back to
the channel today we're going to talk
about mass start events such as
sportives fondos special occasion rides
they are growing in popularity in
attendance in our sport cyclists these
days are attending these events for
varied reasons some for a change of
scenery and a new ride experience others
still for the challenge of riding a
different type of terrain some for the
opportunity to show support to a cause
or an organization so depending on how
the event is approached and your
expectations i always say
that peace in life is determined by your
ability to manage expectations it can be
either a really good experience or one
you would soon like to forget needless
to say these events will differ from
your weekly group ride in a number of
ways that should definitely be noted now
there are usually a very large number of
cyclists with varied skill levels and
riding habits and what you're used to on
your weekly group ride number two
oftentimes these events take place out
of town so it's fair to say that you're
going to be riding in a different venue
therefore you're navigating different
type of ride profile and the profiles
can kind of like be broken down into
like
flat rides rolling terrain or sustained
climbs
lastly the structure of the ride could
be different than what you're used to
meaning when they take their breaks how
they go out the pace the way they deal
with single double pace line
or riding in a peloton all of those
things could be very different okay
my experience with master art events is
very diverse not only have i attended a
lot of different types of events and
written in a lot of different types of
events i've also been involved in them
in a behind the scenes kind of way i've
been involved in route planning route
design logistical planning from the very
beginning of the planning of the event
i've also been involved in sag structure
sag support event support
and i've ran sag
and i've actually even been involved in
pacing a pace group at events so in
today's video i will share four tips
that can help make mast art events a
memorable experience for you beginning
with tip number one plan ahead you've
got to do your homework familiarize
yourself with the route the distance the
elevation make sure that you have the
fitness to complete the course there are
usually multiple distance and speed
options there's going to be a short
distance a medium distance and a long
option typically and there's also going
to be varied speed options like
moderate they don't say slow but they
mean slow
moderate intermediate and fast or
unlimited and they'll usually advertise
like a rolling speed like they'll say
the rolling pace is going to be 18-21
that's not necessarily your average
speed for the event that's just the
rolling speed
for the period of time that you're going
to be on the bike during the event
next plan for weather changes make sure
that you have kit to deal with slight
variations in temperature as well as
unexpected rain in terms of temperature
i say plan for variations of 10 to 15
degrees okay
next part of planning ahead is if you're
planning to travel with your bike you're
either transporting it by car or you're
flying to the event with your bike and
flying with your bike uh man
that's a whole video all by itself
so make sure that you're comfortable
with that process if you're doing it
make sure that you have a plan for
assembly and disassembly of your bike
also make sure that your bike is tuned
and ready with the right type of gearing
for the type of course that you're going
to be riding
the alternative to traveling with your
bike is to renting a bike i recommend if
you do that stick to the big concept
stores
such as the specialized treks the giants
reserve your rental in size and style
online with a credit card or debit card
so that you have some sort of recourse
or accountability
you don't want any surprises there like
you spoke to bob and bob wrote it down
on a notebook but the day you get there
bob hasn't been to work for the last two
days because he's sick and nobody at the
store knows what you're talking about
and you end up with no bike
not cool okay make sure that you also
bring your pedals your favorite saddle
and don't forget to pack your water
bottles that's a big deal
lastly know what type of sag or rest
stop support is offered at the event
that's a big deal and i'm telling you
from experience
different events plan this stuff
differently okay you need to have a plan
for your nutrition
and your hydration to make sure that you
can get through the event properly okay
tip number two pacing
if you are not there to set some sort of
personal speed record like a sub four or
five hour century or you're not there to
go kom snatching uh you want to try to
look for a group or pace that's suitable
to your fitness
best way to do that is that if there are
pre-events the day before like expos or
pre-rides try to attend to meet others
and see what right options and groups
fit you the last event that i attended
in charlotte north carolina i went to a
pre-ride
and i met a group from a neighboring
city and they we after we talked a while
they were planning to do the ride a
certain way they invited me to write
with them the pace sounded perfect for
me and how i wanted to do you know party
pace so we ended up making friendships
and really really having a good time
supporting and encouraging each other
throughout the ride so don't be a hermit
get out the day before the event so that
you can mingle interact and find a plan
of execution
next part of pacing is that
it's better to start slow and finish
strong than to go out fast and then be
crawling across the finish line it's not
a good look cramping is not a good look
probably the only thing worse than
crashing is finishing out the event in a
sag support
that does not lend for a memorable
experience
my philosophy is i hang back and i ride
at a comfortable pace and i enjoy the
people in the atmosphere now if you
decide that you want to go ahead and
kill it go ahead and kill it but make
sure that you have a plan to be able to
get through this thing uh properly okay
tip number three be careful when riding
in groups especially groups where you're
not familiar with the cycles don't just
trust any wheel automatically
observe and assess before you let your
guards down next respect the passing
trains and pace lines
if you're riding along at 21 and a group
is going by you man let them pass you
cleanly if you're like that looks like
fun i'd rather ride with them okay cool
hop into the back try not to jump into
the middle because if you jump into the
middle somebody's got to adjust their
speed to let you in that creates a
ripple effect somebody has to slow down
you don't want to be that guy next uh
respect traffic laws be smart be safe
also respect the environment trash from
uh wrappers and snacks put them in your
jersey pocket until you can dispose of
them properly next
go easy on the selfies
man during the ride there's usually some
sort of photographer or something like
that that's taking pictures of you so if
you're not comfortable with the whole
one-handed riding camera thing save your
picture taking skills to the rest stops
and the post ride events
remember guys it's very important for
you to be safe at these types of events
and tip number quattro
number four is have fun
man cycling lets you see the beauty of
creation in a very unique way different
than when you're driving in a car or
even if you're hiking because cycling
brings you to a level and a pace where
you get to take it in in a very unique
way so anyway those are my four tips for
you to have
a memorable ride experience at mass
start events remember guys our focus
here the channel is to inform instruct
inspire you can help us in doing that by
giving a like or subscribing to the
channel
it's the best way that i can think of
that you can help us mess around with
the youtube algorithm and show us your
support alright thanks for tuning in
guys be blessed

videos.curation_reason: Recent race recap with specific course details and race experience from Dade City Gran Fondo.

quotes.text: What I think is really interesting of this fondo is that it's the time doesn't count from the start to finish. There's actually for the 100 miles, there is four segments and that's what counts on these segments. So, it's nice because you can when you're out of the segment, you can ride at a comfortable pace, enjoy the ride, enjoy the scenery. Then when you get in the segment, that's when you do the effort.

Started pretty cold today, but the day is going to be beautiful and it's going to warm up pretty quickly. So, we're going to start our ride in around 40 something, 45, 46° and we might end our ride close to the 70s. So, big temperature difference there.

There were a lot of these sections where it was just like a single Road through canopies and tunnels of trees and beautiful beautiful landscapes to ride through. Here we are in the tunnel of trees of Dade City look at that that pretty right it's almost like The Bridges of Madison County over here.

key_challenges.name: First segment
key_challenges.description: Much harder than expected, with legs feeling super stiff due to insufficient warm-up time from cold start and descents before the timed segment.

key_challenges.name: Wind conditions
key_challenges.description: Super windy conditions during race weekend, with wind expected to get worse as the morning progresses on race day.

terrain_notes.text: Starts with descents in the first 3 miles before the first timed segment, making it difficult to warm up properly on cold mornings.

gear_mentions.text: Riding 28mm Pirelli P Zero 5000 tires tubeless with 48/35 chainrings and 10-36 cassette for the 100-mile distance.

race_day_tips.text: Race has four timed segments over 100 miles where you can ride comfortably between segments and push hard only during the timed portions, making pacing strategy important.
Start is in 5-minute waves of about 20 riders from 8:00 AM to 10:00 AM rather than a mass start, beginning around 8:40 AM.
Temperature can vary dramatically from mid-40s at start to near 70s at finish, plan clothing strategy accordingly.
Allow extra time beyond the initial 3 miles to properly warm up before the first timed segment, especially in cold conditions.

additional_quotes.text: It's nice because you can when you're out of the segment, you can ride at a comfortable pace, enjoy the ride, enjoy the scenery. Then when you get in the segment, that's when you do the effort.
There's the overall classification and positioning and also by age group. You can qualify for the National Championship of fondos.

rider_intel.search_text: Gran Fondo Florida in Dade City features a unique format with four timed segments over 100 miles where riders can pace comfortably between segments and push hard only during timed portions. The race starts in 5-minute waves of 20 riders from 8:00-10:00 AM rather than a mass start, with over 500 participants competing for overall and age group classifications. Riders can qualify for National Championship events through this race. Key challenges include very windy conditions that worsen throughout the morning, and cold starts in the mid-40s that warm to near 70 degrees by finish. The course begins with descents in the first 3 miles before the first timed segment, making proper warm-up difficult in cold conditions. Riders report the first segment being much harder than expected due to insufficient warm-up time. The terrain includes bike path sections and requires strategy for connecting with other riders during timed segments.
